title: Practice Test - AWS Certified CloudOps Engineer Associate

questions.question:
A company runs high frequency transactional workloads on a single Amazon RDS DB instance. The application commits transactions multiple times per minute. The company now requires automatic failover in the event of infrastructure failure and must ensure that no committed transactions are lost.

Which solution should a systems administrator implement to meet these requirements?
questions.simplifiedQuestion: Which RDS feature provides automatic failover with zero data loss for committed transactions?
choices: Convert the DB instance to a Multi-AZ deployment to enable synchronous replication and automatic failover
Enable automated backups with a short backup retention period and perform a point in time restore to a new DB instance during a disaster recovery event
Configure an RDS read replica in the same Region and automate promotion to primary by using an AWS Lambda function during a failure event
Configure an RDS read replica in a secondary Region and automate promotion to primary by using an AWS Lambda function during a failure event
correctAnswers: Convert the DB instance to a Multi-AZ deployment to enable synchronous replication and automatic failover
questions.explanation: Multi-AZ deployments for Amazon RDS use synchronous replication to maintain a standby instance in a separate Availability Zone. When the primary instance fails, RDS automatically performs a failover to the standby. Because replication is synchronous, committed transactions are written to both the primary and standby before acknowledgment. This ensures that no committed transactions are lost during failover. Multi-AZ provides automatic failover without manual intervention or custom scripting. It is specifically designed for high availability and disaster recovery within a Region.

questions.question:
A CloudOps Engineer is configuring Amazon EC2 status check alarm to publish a notification to an SNS topic when the instance fails either the instance check or system status check.

Which CloudWatch metric is the right choice for this configuration?
questions.simplifiedQuestion: Which CloudWatch metric covers both the instance status check and the system status check?
choices: StatusCheckFailed_Instance
StatusCheckFailed
CombinedStatusCheckFailed
StatusCheckFailed_System
correctAnswers: StatusCheckFailed
questions.explanation: The AWS/EC2 namespace includes a few status check metrics. StatusCheckFailed reports whether the instance has passed both the instance status check and the system status check in the last minute. This metric can be either 0 (passed) or 1 (failed). By default, this metric is available at a 1-minute frequency at no charge.

questions.question:
You are provisioning an internal full LAMP stack using CloudFormation, and the EC2 instance gets configured automatically using the cfn helper scripts, such as cfn-init and cfn-signal. The stack creation fails as CloudFormation fails to receive a signal from your EC2 instance.

What are the possible reasons for this? (Select two)
questions.simplifiedQuestion: Why might CloudFormation fail to receive a cfn-signal from an EC2 instance in a private subnet? (Select two)
choices: The cfn-signal script does not get executed before the timeout of the wait condition
The EC2 instance does not have a proper IAM role allowing to signal the success to CloudFormation
The cfn-init script failed
The subnet where the application is deployed does not have a network route to the CloudFormation service through a NAT Gateway or Internet Gateway
AWS is experiencing an Insufficient Capacity for the instance type you requested
correctAnswers: The cfn-signal script does not get executed before the timeout of the wait condition
The subnet where the application is deployed does not have a network route to the CloudFormation service through a NAT Gateway or Internet Gateway
questions.explanation:
Subnet route: As the use-case mentions an internal full LAMP stack, this implies that the stack is to be deployed in a private subnet. This private subnet must have a network route to the CloudFormation service through a NAT Gateway or Internet Gateway.

Timeout: The Timeout property determines how long AWS CloudFormation waits for the requisite number of success signals. The stack creation can fail if the Timeout property is set to a low value and the signal is not sent in time.

questions.question:
As a CloudOps Engineer, you have been tasked to generate a report on all API calls made for Elastic Load Balancer from the AWS Management Console.

Which feature/service will you use to fetch this data?
questions.simplifiedQuestion: Which AWS service logs all API calls made to Elastic Load Balancing?
choices: Load Balancer Access logs
CloudWatch metrics
CloudTrail logs
Load Balancer Request tracing
correctAnswers: CloudTrail logs
questions.explanation: Elastic Load Balancing is integrated with AWS CloudTrail, a service that provides a record of actions taken by a user, role, or an AWS service in Elastic Load Balancing. CloudTrail captures all API calls for Elastic Load Balancing as events, including calls from the AWS Management Console and code calls to the API operations.

questions.question:
You host a forum for law questions and per your country's law, you must store all the archives of conversations (about 1 TB) every week for 7 years. These archives must not be tampered with in any way, and you must prove you have set enough controls around your data protection.

What should you do?
questions.simplifiedQuestion: How do you store archives in AWS with tamper-proof, write-once-read-many (WORM) compliance for 7 years?
choices: Store the archives in AWS Artifact and enable compliance monitoring
Store the archives in Glacier and set up a Vault Lock Policy for WORM access
Store the archives in S3 and set up a bucket policy, enable versioning and MFA-Delete
Store the archives in EBS and use Linux file system protection on the files
correctAnswers: Store the archives in Glacier and set up a Vault Lock Policy for WORM access
questions.explanation: S3 Glacier Vault Lock allows you to easily deploy and enforce compliance controls for individual S3 Glacier vaults with a vault lock policy. You can specify controls such as "write once read many" (WORM) in a vault lock policy and lock the policy from future edits. Once locked, the policy can no longer be changed, ensuring the archives are not tampered with.

questions.question:
A CloudOps engineer must monitor charges specifically for data transfer between AWS Regions. The engineer needs an automated alerting mechanism that notifies an email distribution list when these data transfer charges reach 75 percent of a predefined spending threshold. The solution must require minimal operational effort to set up and maintain.

Which solution should the CloudOps engineer implement to meet these requirements?
questions.simplifiedQuestion: How do you set up automated email alerts when inter-Region data transfer costs hit 75% of a budget?
choices: Create an Amazon CloudWatch alarm for estimated charges and set the alarm threshold to 75 percent of the target amount. Publish the alarm notification to an Amazon Simple Notification Service (Amazon SNS) topic and subscribe the email distribution list to the topic

Configure AWS Cost Anomaly Detection to monitor data transfer related cost patterns. Create an anomaly subscription that notifies an Amazon Simple Notification Service (Amazon SNS) topic. Subscribe the email distribution list to the topic and treat anomaly alerts as the 75 percent early warning signal

Schedule an AWS Lambda function to call the AWS Cost Explorer API and retrieve inter-Region data transfer costs. Compare the returned amount to 75 percent of the defined threshold and send an email to the distribution list by using Amazon Simple Email Service (Amazon SES)

Create a cost budget in AWS Budgets that filters to inter-Region data transfer charges. Configure an alert that triggers at 75 percent of the budgeted amount and send notifications directly to the email distribution list
correctAnswers: Create a cost budget in AWS Budgets that filters to inter-Region data transfer charges. Configure an alert that triggers at 75 percent of the budgeted amount and send notifications directly to the email distribution list
questions.explanation: AWS Budgets is the native AWS service designed to track costs against defined thresholds and send alerts when spend reaches configured limits. By creating a cost budget and filtering to the relevant data transfer charge dimensions, the CloudOps engineer can monitor inter-Region data transfer costs without building custom analytics pipelines or polling jobs.

questions.question:
An ecommerce platform uses Amazon ElastiCache for Redis OSS to cache frequently accessed product query results. CloudWatch metrics show a consistently high number of cache evictions. As a CloudOps Engineer, you have been tasked to reduce evictions and ensure that popular items remain in cache, while minimizing operational complexity.

Which solution do you recommend?
questions.simplifiedQuestion: How do you reduce high cache evictions in ElastiCache for Redis with minimal effort?
choices: Decrease the default TTL setting for cached product entries to free memory more quickly
Replace the existing cluster with a new ElastiCache cluster that uses a larger node instance type to provide more memory
Increase the default TTL setting for cached product entries so that data remains in memory longer
Enable Multi-AZ with automatic failover on the Redis replication group to reduce eviction events during peak traffic
correctAnswers: Replace the existing cluster with a new ElastiCache cluster that uses a larger node instance type to provide more memory
questions.explanation: Cache evictions in Redis typically occur when memory usage reaches the configured maximum and Redis must remove keys based on the eviction policy. Increasing available memory directly reduces eviction pressure. Migrating to a larger node type increases memory capacity without requiring changes to application logic or key management strategies. This ensures that frequently accessed data remains in cache longer under high load.

questions.question:
A company hosts a web application on Amazon EC2 instances behind an Elastic Load Balancer and manages capacity by using an Auto Scaling group. The application meets performance requirements for most of the day but experiences consistent performance degradation during a predictable two hour daily traffic spike. A CloudOps engineer must improve application performance during this peak window while minimizing ongoing operational effort.

Which solution should the CloudOps engineer implement?
questions.simplifiedQuestion: How should you pre-scale an Auto Scaling group for a predictable daily traffic spike?
choices: Increase the minimum desired capacity of the Auto Scaling group to match the number of instances required during the daily peak traffic window
Define a scheduled scaling action that increases the Auto Scaling group capacity shortly before the expected traffic spike and decreases capacity after the peak period ends
Enable predictive scaling for the Auto Scaling group and allow Amazon EC2 Auto Scaling to forecast capacity needs and adjust the group automatically
Configure an Application Auto Scaling target tracking policy that scales on average CPU utilization and rely on reactive scaling to handle the traffic spike
correctAnswers: Define a scheduled scaling action that increases the Auto Scaling group capacity shortly before the expected traffic spike and decreases capacity after the peak period ends
questions.explanation: Scheduled scaling is designed for workloads with predictable traffic patterns that occur at known times. By defining a scheduled action, the CloudOps engineer can ensure that additional EC2 instances are launched before the two hour peak traffic window begins, preventing performance degradation during the surge. This approach avoids the delay associated with reactive scaling policies.

questions.question:
A company uses AWS Organizations and recently deployed AWS Control Tower to manage its multi account environment. The company now wants to centralize identity management by federating AWS IAM Identity Center with an external SAML 2.0 identity provider. The solution must enable centralized access to all AWS accounts and supported cloud applications.

Which prerequisites must a CloudOps engineer have to successfully configure this federation? (Select two)
questions.simplifiedQuestion: What do you need to federate IAM Identity Center with an external SAML 2.0 IdP? (Select two)
choices: Obtain the SAML metadata document generated by AWS IAM Identity Center for use when configuring the external identity provider
Collect the SAML metadata from the external identity provider, including the public X.509 signing certificate
Enable AWS Directory Service for Microsoft Active Directory in the management account and establish a trust relationship with the external identity provider
Configure AWS IAM roles in each member account with trust policies that reference the external SAML identity provider before enabling federation in IAM Identity Center
Retrieve the static IP address of the external identity provider to allow network based trust configuration
correctAnswers: Obtain the SAML metadata document generated by AWS IAM Identity Center for use when configuring the external identity provider
Collect the SAML metadata from the external identity provider, including the public X.509 signing certificate
questions.explanation:
IAM Identity Center Metadata: AWS IAM Identity Center acts as a SAML service provider. To establish trust, the external IdP must be configured with the IAM Identity Center SAML metadata, which defines the service provider entity ID and endpoints.

External IdP Metadata: To establish trust, AWS IAM Identity Center must trust assertions issued by that IdP. The engineer must obtain the IdP metadata, which includes the IdP entity ID, SAML endpoints, and the public X.509 certificate used to sign assertions.

questions.question:
A retail company wants to get out of the business of owning and maintaining its own IT infrastructure. As part of this digital transformation, the company wants to archive about 5PB of data in its on-premises data center to durable long term storage.

As a CloudOps Engineer, what is your recommendation to migrate this data in the MOST cost-optimal way?
questions.simplifiedQuestion: What is the most cost-effective way to migrate 5PB of on-premises data to Glacier?
choices: Setup AWS direct connect between the on-premises data center and AWS Cloud. Use this connection to transfer the data into AWS Glacier
Transfer the on-premises data into multiple Snowball Edge Storage Optimized devices. Copy the Snowball Edge data into AWS Glacier
Setup Site-to-Site VPN connection between the on-premises data center and AWS Cloud. Use this connection to transfer the data into AWS Glacier
Transfer the on-premises data into multiple Snowball Edge Storage Optimized devices. Copy the Snowball Edge data into Amazon S3 and create a lifecycle policy to transition the data into AWS Glacier
correctAnswers: Transfer the on-premises data into multiple Snowball Edge Storage Optimized devices. Copy the Snowball Edge data into Amazon S3 and create a lifecycle policy to transition the data into AWS Glacier
questions.explanation: Snowball Edge Storage Optimized is the optimal choice to securely and quickly transfer dozens of terabytes to petabytes of data to AWS. The data stored on the Snowball Edge device can be copied into an S3 bucket and later transitioned into AWS Glacier via a lifecycle policy. You can't directly copy data from Snowball Edge devices into AWS Glacier.

questions.question:
Your home-cooking website stores its recipes and comments from users in a Multi-AZ RDS database, which is located in a private subnet. As of yesterday, it seems that your users are unable to access the website and see an error message "512 - Cannot connect to the database".

What could be the reason why the website cannot connect to the database anymore? (Select three)
questions.simplifiedQuestion: What can cause an RDS database in a private subnet to suddenly become unreachable? (Select three)
choices: DB Security Group inbound rules have changed
Network ACL outbound rules have changed
The primary database's private IP has changed
A read replica has been created recently
Security Group outbound rules have changed
Network ACL inbound rules have changed
correctAnswers: DB Security Group inbound rules have changed
Network ACL outbound rules have changed
Network ACL inbound rules have changed
questions.explanation:
Security Group Inbound: If the DB Security Group inbound rules have changed, they may no longer allow traffic from the web servers.

Network ACL Inbound/Outbound: Network ACLs are stateless. If the inbound or the outbound NACL rules have changed, they may be blocking the connection. Responses to allowed inbound traffic are subject to the rules for outbound traffic (and vice versa).

questions.question:
A company transfers multiple gigabytes of data to Amazon S3 every day from geographically distributed client locations. The company wants to increase upload throughput and reduce latency when transferring large files.

Which solution should a systems administrator implement to meet these requirements?
questions.simplifiedQuestion: How do you speed up S3 uploads from geographically distributed clients?
choices: Activate S3 Transfer Acceleration on the bucket and direct uploads to the transfer acceleration endpoint
Deploy an Amazon ElastiCache cluster to cache frequently uploaded objects before sending them to Amazon S3
Configure AWS Global Accelerator and associate it with the S3 bucket to optimize upload routing
Enable S3 Cross-Region Replication to replicate uploaded objects to a bucket in another Region closer to users
correctAnswers: Activate S3 Transfer Acceleration on the bucket and direct uploads to the transfer acceleration endpoint
questions.explanation: S3 Transfer Acceleration enables faster uploads to Amazon S3 by routing traffic through the AWS global edge network. When clients upload data using the acceleration endpoint, the data travels over optimized network paths to the nearest edge location and then traverses the AWS backbone to the target bucket. This reduces latency and improves throughput for large file uploads.

questions.question:
Your data center generates tens of terabytes of data daily and has a cumulative historic data volume of 5PB. The data center is running short of storage as well as bandwidth infrastructure to store or transfer this data. Later you would like to analyze this data using Redshift or Athena, however, first you must clean it using a proprietary process running on EC2.

What's the optimal way of moving this data to the cloud?
questions.simplifiedQuestion: What is the best way to move 5PB of on-premises data to AWS when you also need local compute for pre-processing?
choices: Use Snowball Edge
Use AWS Data Migration
Use S3 transfer acceleration
Use Volume Gateway
correctAnswers: Use Snowball Edge
questions.explanation: Snowball Edge Storage Optimized is the optimal choice if you need to securely and quickly transfer dozens of terabytes to petabytes of data to AWS. It provides up to 80 TB of usable HDD storage, 40 vCPUs, 1 TB of SATA SSD storage, and up to 40 Gb network connectivity to address large scale data transfer and pre-processing use cases.

questions.question:
A team noticed that it has accidentally deleted the AMI of Amazon EC2 instances belonging to the test environment. The team had configured backups via EBS snapshots for these instances.

Which of the following options would you suggest to recover/rebuild the accidentally deleted AMI? (Select two)
questions.simplifiedQuestion: How can you recreate a deleted AMI? (Select two)
choices: Recover the AMI from Amazon EBS snapshots that were created as backups before the deletion of AMI
Create a new AMI from Amazon EC2 instances that were launched before the deletion of AMI
Recover the AMI from the current Amazon EC2 instances that were launched before the deletion of AMI
AWS Support retains backups of AMIs. Write to the support team to get help for recovering the lost AMI
Create a new AMI from Amazon EBS snapshots that were created as backups
correctAnswers: Create a new AMI from Amazon EBS snapshots that were created as backups
Create a new AMI from Amazon EC2 instances that were launched before the deletion of AMI
questions.explanation:
It isn't possible to restore or recover a deleted or deregistered AMI. However, you can create a new, identical AMI using one of the following:

- Amazon EBS snapshots that were created as backups.
- Amazon EC2 instances that were launched from the deleted AMI.

questions.question: How can you enforce encryption on all the files uploaded into your example S3 bucket?
questions.simplifiedQuestion: How do you enforce server-side encryption on all objects uploaded to an S3 bucket?
choices: Use the following S3 bucket policy: {"Statement":[{"Action": "s3:*","Effect":"Deny","Principal": "*","Resource":"arn:aws:s3:::bucketname/*","Condition":{"Bool":{ "aws:SecureTransport": true }}}]}
Use an encrypted CloudFront distribution in front of your S3 bucket
Use the following S3 bucket policy: {"Statement":[{"Action": "s3:*","Effect":"Deny","Principal": "*","Resource":"arn:aws:s3:::bucketname/*","Condition":{"Bool":{ "aws:SecureTransport": false }}}]}
Using the "Default Encryption" setting in AWS S3
correctAnswers: Using the "Default Encryption" setting in AWS S3
questions.explanation: Amazon S3 default encryption provides a way to set the default encryption behavior for an S3 bucket. You can set default encryption on a bucket so that all new objects are encrypted when they are stored in the bucket. The objects are encrypted using server-side encryption with either Amazon S3-managed keys (SSE-S3) or KMS keys stored in AWS KMS.

questions.question:
As part of the best practices for DevOps, all your infrastructure is deployed using CloudFormation. This includes EBS volumes. When the CloudFormation stacks are deleted, it is mandatory to keep a snapshot of the EBS volumes for backup and compliance purposes.

How can you achieve this using CloudFormation?
questions.simplifiedQuestion: How do you automatically snapshot an EBS volume when its CloudFormation stack is deleted?
choices: Use cfn helper scripts and Wait Conditions upon stack deletion
Reference the EBS volume as a stack output
Enable termination protection
Use DeletionPolicy=Snapshot
correctAnswers: Use DeletionPolicy=Snapshot
questions.explanation: To control how AWS CloudFormation handles the EBS volume when the stack is deleted, set a deletion policy for your volume. Using DeletionPolicy: Snapshot ensures a snapshot of the volume is created before the stack is deleted.

questions.question:
A CloudOps Engineer has just configured an internet facing Load Balancer for traffic distribution across the EC2 instances placed in different Availability Zones. The clients, however, are unable to connect to the Load Balancer.

What is the most plausible reason for this issue?
questions.simplifiedQuestion: Why can't clients connect to an internet-facing Load Balancer?
choices: The target returned the error code of 200 indicating an error on the server side
The target was incorrectly configured as a Lambda function and not an EC2 instance
It is an internal server error
A security group or network ACL is not allowing traffic from the client
correctAnswers: A security group or network ACL is not allowing traffic from the client
questions.explanation: If the load balancer is not responding to client requests, a common issue is that the security group for the load balancer or any network ACLs for the load balancer subnets do not allow inbound traffic from the clients or outbound traffic to the clients on the listener ports.

questions.question:
Security and Compliance is a Shared Responsibility between AWS and the customer. As part of this Shared Responsibility, the customer is also responsible for securing the resources that he has procured under his AWS account.

Which of the following is the responsibility of the customer?
questions.simplifiedQuestion: Under the AWS Shared Responsibility Model, which task is the customer's responsibility?
choices: AWS is responsible for training their customers and their employees as part of Customer Specific training
For Amazon S3 service, managing the operating system and platform is customer responsibility
AWS is responsible for patching and fixing flaws within the infrastructure, for patching the guest Operating Systems and applications of the customers
For Amazon EC2 service, managing guest operating system (including updates and security patches), application software and Security Groups is the responsibility of the customer
correctAnswers: For Amazon EC2 service, managing guest operating system (including updates and security patches), application software and Security Groups is the responsibility of the customer
questions.explanation: For IaaS services like EC2, the customer is responsible for managing the guest operating system (including updates and security patches), any application software or utilities installed on the instances, and the configuration of the AWS-provided firewall (security group).

questions.question:
You distribute a monthly raw data extract of your public forum's discussions that is about 10TB each month. Currently, the archive is distributed through an EFS drive, that is mounted on all your EC2 instances. Customers retrieve the file through the load balancer you have. This solution is costing you a lot of money and forces you to tremendously scale on the 1st of each month as people all try to retrieve the file at the same time.

What can you do to improve the situation?
questions.simplifiedQuestion: How do you cost-effectively distribute a large static file to many concurrent users?
choices: Store the files in S3 and distribute them using a CloudFront distribution instead
Store the files on instance stores instead, so you don't need to use EFS anymore
Enable static file caching on the ALB
Enable enhanced networking between EC2 and ALB
correctAnswers: Store the files in S3 and distribute them using a CloudFront distribution instead
questions.explanation: S3 is more cost-effective than EFS. Further, storing static content with S3 and using CloudFront as a CDN delivers the content securely and at scale. By design, delivering data out of CloudFront can be more cost-effective than delivering it from S3 directly.

questions.question:
You just released a new mobile game and users have the chance to interact with each other. In order to publish a profile picture, your company has made the architectural decision to have users directly upload their images into a designated S3 bucket.

How can you provide write access to the mobile application users effectively?
questions.simplifiedQuestion: How do you securely give mobile app users write access to an S3 bucket?
choices: Federate the users with SAML so they can use Single Sign-On (SSO) to access S3
Create an AWS Lambda function that will create an IAM User for each new user, and store their API keys in the mobile app database
Federate the users with Cognito so they can assume a role to access S3
Create one IAM user and publish the access keys as part of the mobile application
correctAnswers: Federate the users with Cognito so they can assume a role to access S3
questions.explanation: Amazon Cognito lets you add user sign-up, sign-in, and access control to your mobile apps quickly and easily. Application-specific user authentication can be provided via a Cognito User Pool and then users can access AWS services such as S3 using a Cognito Identity Pool.

questions.question:
As CloudOps Engineer, you have created two configuration files for CloudWatch Agent configuration. The first configuration file collects a set of metrics and logs from all servers and the second configuration file collects metrics from certain applications. You have given the same name to both the files but stored these files in different file paths.

What is the outcome when the CloudWatch Agent is started with the first configuration file and then the second configuration file is appended to it?
questions.simplifiedQuestion: What happens when you use append-config with a CloudWatch Agent config file that has the same filename as an existing one?
choices: Second configuration file parameters are added to the Agent already running with the first configuration file parameters
Two different Agents are started with different configurations, collecting the metrics and logs listed in either of the configuration files
A CloudWatch Agent can have only one configuration file and all required parameters are defined in this file alone
The append command overwrites the information from the first configuration file instead of appending to it
correctAnswers: The append command overwrites the information from the first configuration file instead of appending to it
questions.explanation: If you use append-config with a configuration file with the same file name as a configuration file that the agent is already using, the append command overwrites the information from the first configuration file instead of appending to it. This is true even if the two configuration files with the same file name are on different file paths.

questions.question:
As a service provider, you generate a daily report that you need to share with your dynamically changing list of over 10,000 customers. These reports sit in S3, and you would like to automate sharing the reports with them so they can have on-demand access upon their identity being proven.

You plan to use Cognito, API Gateway and AWS Lambda to address this use-case. On the S3 side, what should you do?
questions.simplifiedQuestion: How do you securely share private S3 files with authenticated users on demand?
choices: Provide each of your customers an AWS user and tell them to use the CLI
Create a bucket policy so that the S3 files are only accessible from CloudFront and force SSL mutual authentication there
Generate pre-signed URLs for your reports
Make the S3 bucket public and password protect each S3 file. Share the password with each customer
correctAnswers: Generate pre-signed URLs for your reports
questions.explanation: A presigned URL gives you access to the object identified in the URL, provided that the creator of the presigned URL has permissions to access that object. The object owner can share objects with others by creating a presigned URL, using their own security credentials, to grant time-limited permission to download the objects.

questions.question:
Your website is hosted on S3 and exposed through a CloudFront distribution and some users are said to experience a lot of 501 errors.

How can you analyze these errors and come up with a solution?
questions.simplifiedQuestion: How do you analyze HTTP 501 errors on a CloudFront + S3 hosted website?
choices: Analyze the CloudFront access logs using Athena
Analyze the CloudFront access logs using Inspector
Enable S3 access logs and analyze using Athena
Enable S3 access logs and analyze using Inspector
correctAnswers: Analyze the CloudFront access logs using Athena
questions.explanation: You can configure CloudFront to create access log files that contain detailed information about every user request. AWS recommends that you use these logs to understand the nature of the requests for your content. Amazon Athena is an interactive query service that makes it easy to analyze data directly in Amazon S3 using standard SQL.

questions.question:
An e-commerce company runs its web application on Amazon EC2 instances backed by Amazon Elastic Block Store (Amazon EBS) volumes. An Amazon S3 bucket is used for storing sharable data. A developer has attached an Amazon EBS to an Amazon EC2 instance, but it's still in the "attaching" state after 10-15 minutes.

As a CloudOps Engineer, what solution will you suggest to fix this issue with the EBS volume?
questions.simplifiedQuestion: Why is an EBS volume stuck in the "attaching" state, and how do you fix it?
choices: Each EBS volume receives an initial I/O credit balance, an error in accumulating the credit balance can stop the volume from attaching properly to the instance. Restart the instance to fix the error
The EBS volume could be encrypted and the custom KMS key used to encrypt the snapshot is missing. The custom KMS key needs to be added to the volume configuration
Check that the device name you specified when you attempted to attach the EBS volume isn't already in use. Attempt to attach the volume to the instance, again, but use a different device name

The attaching status indicates that the underlying hardware related to your EBS volume has failed. This issue cannot be fixed. Raise a service request on AWS and request for a new volume. You are not charged for volumes that are in error state
correctAnswers: Check that the device name you specified when you attempted to attach the EBS volume isn't already in use. Attempt to attach the volume to the instance, again, but use a different device name
questions.explanation: If the specified device name is already being used by the block device driver of the EC2 instance, the operation fails and the EBS volume is stuck in the attaching state. You can resolve this by force detaching the volume and attempting to attach it again using a different device name.

questions.question:
An e-commerce company is running its server infrastructure on Amazon EC2 instance store-backed instances. For better performance, the company has decided to move their applications to another Amazon EC2 instance store-backed instance with a different instance type.

How will you configure a solution for this requirement?
questions.simplifiedQuestion: How do you change the instance type of an instance store-backed EC2 instance?
choices: Create an image of your instance, and then launch a new instance from this image with the instance type that you need. Take any Elastic IP address that you've associated with your original instance and associate it with the new instance for uninterrupted service to your application
You can't resize an instance store-backed instance. Instead, you choose a new compatible instance and move your application to the new instance

Create an image of your instance, and then launch a new instance from this image with the instance type that you need. Any public IP address associated with the instance can be moved with the instance for uninterrupted access of services
You can't resize an instance store-backed instance. Instead, configure an EBS volume to be the root device for the instance and migrate using the EBS volume
correctAnswers: Create an image of your instance, and then launch a new instance from this image with the instance type that you need. Take any Elastic IP address that you've associated with your original instance and associate it with the new instance for uninterrupted service to your application
questions.explanation: To move your application from one instance store-backed instance to another with a different instance type, you must create an AMI from your instance, and then launch a new instance from this AMI. To ensure uninterrupted service, you must take any Elastic IP address associated with the original instance and associate it with the new instance.

questions.question:
After a developer had mistakenly shutdown a test instance, the Team Lead has decided to configure termination protection on all the instances. As a CloudOps Engineer, you have been tasked to review the termination policy and check its viability for the given requirements.

Which of the following choices are correct about Amazon EC2 instance's termination policy (Select two)?
questions.simplifiedQuestion: Which statements about EC2 termination protection are correct? (Select two)
choices: To prevent instances that are part of an Auto Scaling group from terminating on scale in, use instance protection
The DisableApiTermination attribute prevents Amazon EC2 Auto Scaling from terminating an instance
The DisableApiTermination attribute prevents you from terminating an instance by initiating shutdown from the instance
You can't enable termination protection for Spot Instances
The DisableApiTermination attribute does not prevent you from terminating an instance by initiating shutdown from Amazon EC2 console
correctAnswers: To prevent instances that are part of an Auto Scaling group from terminating on scale in, use instance protection
You can't enable termination protection for Spot Instances
questions.explanation:
Auto Scaling: The DisableApiTermination attribute does not prevent Amazon EC2 Auto Scaling from terminating an instance. For instances in an Auto Scaling group, use instance protection to prevent termination on scale in.

Spot Instances: You can't enable termination protection for Spot Instances, as they are terminated when the Spot price exceeds your bid.

questions.question:
A systems administrator observes that an Amazon CloudFront distribution has a cache hit ratio below 10 percent. The low cache efficiency is increasing origin load and degrading user performance.

Which actions should the administrator take to improve cache efficiency, enhance network performance, and reduce load on the origin? (Select two)
questions.simplifiedQuestion: How do you improve a CloudFront distribution's low cache hit ratio? (Select two)
choices: Enable CloudFront Origin Shield in the Region closest to the origin to consolidate cache misses before they reach the origin
Configure an origin group with two origins and enable origin failover to distribute traffic across both origins
Configure Amazon Route 53 weighted routing policies to distribute user traffic across multiple CloudFront distributions
Enable automatic compression in the cache behavior configuration for supported file types
Increase the default and maximum TTL (Time to Live) settings in the cache behavior to allow objects to remain cached longer at edge locations
correctAnswers: Enable CloudFront Origin Shield in the Region closest to the origin to consolidate cache misses before they reach the origin
Increase the default and maximum TTL (Time to Live) settings in the cache behavior to allow objects to remain cached longer at edge locations
questions.explanation:
Origin Shield: Origin Shield introduces an additional centralized caching layer between edge locations and the origin. Cache misses from multiple edge locations are consolidated through this layer, reducing duplicate requests to the origin.

TTL Values: Increasing default and maximum TTL values allows content to remain cached longer at edge locations, reducing the frequency of origin fetches and increasing the cache hit ratio.

questions.question:
As a CloudOps Engineer, you have been asked to fix the network performance issues for a fleet of Amazon EC2 instances of a company.

Which of the following use-cases represents the right fit for using enhanced networking?
questions.simplifiedQuestion: When should you use enhanced networking on EC2 instances?
choices: To configure multi-attach for an EBS volume that can be attached to a maximum of 16 EC2 instances in a single Availability Zone
To support throughput near or exceeding 20K packets per second (PPS) on the VIF driver
To configure Direct Connect to reach speeds up to 25 Gbps between EC2 instances
To reach speeds up to 2,500 Gbps between EC2 instances
correctAnswers: To support throughput near or exceeding 20K packets per second (PPS) on the VIF driver
questions.explanation: Enhanced networking uses SR-IOV to provide high-performance networking. It provides higher bandwidth, higher PPS performance, and consistently lower latencies. If your throughput is near or exceeding 20K PPS on the VIF driver, it's a best practice to use enhanced networking.

questions.question:
The development team at an IT company is looking at moving its web applications to Amazon EC2 instances. The team is weighing its options for EBS volumes and instance store-backed instances for these applications with varied workloads.

Which of the following would you identify as correct regarding instance store and EBS volumes? (Select three)
questions.simplifiedQuestion: Which statements about EBS volumes and instance store are correct? (Select three)
choices: Snapshots of EBS volumes, stored on Amazon S3, can be accessed using Amazon S3 APIs
EBS encryption does not support boot volumes
By default, data on a non-root EBS volume is preserved even if the instance is shutdown or terminated
Use separate Amazon EBS volumes for the operating system and your data, even though root volume persistence feature is available

Data stored in the instance store is preserved when you stop or terminate your instance. However, data is lost when you hibernate the instance. Configure EBS volumes or have a backup plan to avoid using critical data to this behavior
EBS snapshots only capture data that has been written to your Amazon EBS volume, which might exclude any data that has been locally cached by your application or operating system
correctAnswers: By default, data on a non-root EBS volume is preserved even if the instance is shutdown or terminated
Use separate Amazon EBS volumes for the operating system and your data, even though root volume persistence feature is available
EBS snapshots only capture data that has been written to your Amazon EBS volume, which might exclude any data that has been locally cached by your application or operating system
questions.explanation:
Non-root EBS Volumes: By default, when you attach a non-root EBS volume, its DeleteOnTermination attribute is set to false, so the data is preserved.

Separate Volumes: AWS recommends using separate volumes for the OS and data so that the data volume persists even after instance termination.

Snapshots: Snapshots only capture data written to the volume, potentially excluding locally cached data. For consistency, detach the volume or shut down the instance before taking a snapshot.

questions.question:
A developer is tasked with cleaning up obsolete resources. When he tried to delete an AWS CloudFormation stack, the stack deletion process returned without any error or a success message. The stack was not deleted either.

What is the reason for this behavior and how will you fix it?
questions.simplifiedQuestion: Why does a CloudFormation stack deletion silently fail with no error message?
choices: The AWS user who initiated the stack deletion does not have enough permissions
Some resources must be empty before they can be deleted. Such resources will not be deleted if they are not empty and stack deletion fails without any error
Dependent resources should be deleted first, before deleting the rest of the resources in the stack. If this order is not followed, then stack deletion fails without an error
If you attempt to delete a stack with termination protection enabled, the deletion fails and the stack - including its status - remains unchanged
correctAnswers: If you attempt to delete a stack with termination protection enabled, the deletion fails and the stack - including its status - remains unchanged
questions.explanation: You cannot delete stacks that have termination protection enabled. If you attempt to do so, the deletion fails and the stack's status remains unchanged. You must disable termination protection on the stack first, then perform the delete operation again.

questions.question:
A banking service uses Amazon EC2 instances and Amazon RDS databases to run its core business functionalities. The Chief Technology Officer (CTO) of the company has requested granular OS level metrics from the database service for benchmarking.

As a CloudOps Engineer, how will you provide this information?
questions.simplifiedQuestion: How do you get granular OS-level metrics from an RDS database instance?
choices: Enable Enhanced Monitoring for your RDS DB instance
Enable Performance Insights to expand on the existing Amazon RDS monitoring features to illustrate your database's performance
Subscribe to CloudWatch metrics that track CPU utilization of the instances the RDS is hosted on
Subscribe to Amazon RDS events to be notified when changes occur with a DB instance and its connected resources
correctAnswers: Enable Enhanced Monitoring for your RDS DB instance
questions.explanation: Amazon RDS provides metrics in real time for the operating system (OS) that your DB instance runs on through Enhanced Monitoring. Enhanced Monitoring metrics are useful when you want to see how different processes or threads on a DB instance use the CPU. These metrics are ingested by Amazon CloudWatch Logs as log entries.

questions.question:
A company initially used a manual process to create and manage different IAM roles needed for the organization. As the company expanded and lines of business grew, different AWS accounts were created to manage the AWS resources as well as the users. The manual process has resulted in errors with IAM roles getting created with insufficient permissions. The company is looking at automating the process of creating and managing the necessary IAM roles for multiple AWS accounts. The company already uses AWS Organizations to manage multiple AWS accounts.

As a CloudOps Engineer, can you suggest an effective way to automate this process?
questions.simplifiedQuestion: How do you automate deploying IAM roles across multiple AWS accounts in AWS Organizations?
choices: Create CloudFormation templates and reuse them to create necessary IAM roles in each of the AWS accounts
Use AWS Resource Access Manager that integrates with AWS Organizations to deploy and manage shared resources across AWS accounts
Use CloudFormation StackSets with AWS Organizations to deploy and manage IAM roles to multiple AWS accounts simultaneously
Use AWS Directory Service with AWS Organizations to automatically associate necessary IAM roles with the Microsoft Active Directory users
correctAnswers: Use CloudFormation StackSets with AWS Organizations to deploy and manage IAM roles to multiple AWS accounts simultaneously
questions.explanation: CloudFormation StackSets allow you to roll out CloudFormation stacks over multiple AWS accounts and Regions. Using StackSets with AWS Organizations, you can centrally orchestrate deployment of IAM roles across accounts in your organization. A new service-managed permission model automatically configures the necessary IAM permissions.

questions.question:
You are developing a new CloudFormation stack and writing some very complex cfn-init code. The code fails and you would like to debug why. When reading the documentation, you see all the logs are in the file /var/cfn/cfn-init-output.log and will give you more information as to why the instance provisioning is failing. But you realize that you can't gain access to this file as the CloudFormation stack always terminates the EC2 instance when the creation fails.

What can you do to access these logs files, while not changing the way your EC2 instance works and ensuring you can debug your instance over 24 hours?
questions.simplifiedQuestion: How do you prevent CloudFormation from terminating an EC2 instance on stack creation failure so you can debug?
choices: Install the CloudWatch logs agent, create a new IAM role and assign it to the EC2 instance, and send the logs directly to CloudWatch Logs
Increase the Wait Timeout to 2 hours
Set OnFailure=DO_NOTHING
Enable VPC Flow Logs and intercept the cfn-init log file
correctAnswers: Set OnFailure=DO_NOTHING
questions.explanation: You can use the OnFailure property of the CloudFormation CreateStack call for this use-case. Setting OnFailure=DO_NOTHING will prevent the termination of the EC2 instances created by the CloudFormation stack, allowing you to access the log files for debugging.

questions.question:
A company connects its VPC to an on premises data center through a Site-to-Site VPN. An Amazon EC2 instance with private IP address 172.31.17.137 resides in a private subnet. An administrator attempts to ping the instance from an on premises host at 205.0.115.14 but receives no response. VPC Flow Logs show the following entries for the EC2 instance's elastic network interface:

2 123456123456 eni-1234b8za 205.0.115.14 172.31.17.137 0 0 1 4 336 1432919029 1432919129 ACCEPT OK 2 123456123456 eni-1234b8za 172.31.17.137 205.0.115.14 0 0 1 4 336 1432919039 1432919129 REJECT OK

What action should the administrator take to resolve the issue?
questions.simplifiedQuestion: VPC Flow Logs show inbound ACCEPT but outbound REJECT for ICMP ping. What's the fix?
choices: Update the EC2 instance security group to explicitly allow outbound ICMP traffic to the on premises host
Disable source and destination checks on the EC2 instance to allow return traffic to flow back to the on premises host
Modify the subnet network ACL to allow outbound ICMP traffic to the on premises host
Modify the subnet network ACL to allow inbound ICMP traffic from the on premises host
correctAnswers: Modify the subnet network ACL to allow outbound ICMP traffic to the on premises host
questions.explanation: The flow logs show that inbound traffic is accepted, but return traffic is rejected. Network ACLs are stateless, so they require explicit allow rules for both inbound and outbound traffic. The outbound response must be explicitly allowed. Since the inbound rule is already working, an outbound rule for ICMP to the on-premises IP range is needed.

questions.question:
A company wants its architecture team to receive immediate email notifications whenever new Amazon EC2 instances are launched in the primary AWS production account. The notification mechanism must be event driven and require minimal ongoing maintenance.

Which solution should a CloudOps engineer implement to meet these requirements?
questions.simplifiedQuestion: How do you send event-driven email notifications when new EC2 instances are launched?
choices: Embed an email notification script in the EC2 user data that sends a message to the architecture team whenever an instance starts. Enforce the use of this user data script through a standardized EC2 build process

Enable AWS CloudTrail management events and configure an Amazon CloudWatch Logs metric filter to detect instance launch API calls. Create a CloudWatch alarm that sends email notifications directly to the architecture team when the metric threshold is breached

Create an Amazon Simple Notification Service topic with an email subscription for the architecture team. Configure an Amazon EventBridge rule that detects EC2 instance launch events and publishes the events to the SNS topic

Configure AWS Config to record EC2 resource changes and use an AWS Config rule to detect new instances. Set up a remediation action that sends an email notification to the architecture team when a new resource is recorded
correctAnswers: Create an Amazon Simple Notification Service topic with an email subscription for the architecture team. Configure an Amazon EventBridge rule that detects EC2 instance launch events and publishes the events to the SNS topic
questions.explanation: Amazon EventBridge natively integrates with Amazon EC2 and emits events for instance launches. By configuring a rule to match these events and publish them to an SNS topic with email subscriptions, the solution is fully event-driven, requires minimal operational effort, and delivers near real-time notifications.

questions.question:
A retail company has realized that their Amazon EBS volume backed EC2 instance is consistently over-utilized and needs an upgrade. A developer has connected with you to understand the key parameters to be considered when changing the instance type.

As a CloudOps Engineer, which of the following would you identify as correct regarding the instance types for the given use-case? (Select three)
questions.simplifiedQuestion: What should you know when changing the instance type of an EBS-backed EC2 instance? (Select three)
choices: If your instance is in an Auto Scaling group, the Amazon EC2 Auto Scaling service marks the stopped instance as unhealthy, and may terminate it and launch a replacement instance
There is no downtime on the instance if you choose an instance of a compatible type since AWS starts the new instance and shifts the applications from current instance

Resizing of an instance is possible if the root device is either EBS volume or an instance store volume. However, instance store volumes taking longer to start on the new instance, since cache data is lost on these instances
You must stop your Amazon EBS–backed instance before you can change its instance type. AWS moves the instance to new hardware; however, the instance ID does not change
The new instance retains its public, private IPv4 addresses, any Elastic IP addresses, and any IPv6 addresses that were associated with the old instance
Resizing of an instance is only possible if the root device for your instance is an EBS volume
correctAnswers: If your instance is in an Auto Scaling group, the Amazon EC2 Auto Scaling service marks the stopped instance as unhealthy, and may terminate it and launch a replacement instance
You must stop your Amazon EBS–backed instance before you can change its instance type. AWS moves the instance to new hardware; however, the instance ID does not change
Resizing of an instance is only possible if the root device for your instance is an EBS volume
questions.explanation:
Auto Scaling: If the instance is in an Auto Scaling group, stopping it marks it as unhealthy, and Auto Scaling may terminate it. You should suspend scaling processes while resizing.

Stopping Instance: You must stop an EBS-backed instance before changing its type. The instance ID remains the same.

Resizing: Resizing (changing instance type) is only possible if the root device is an EBS volume.

questions.question:
A CloudOps Engineer was asked to enable versioning on an Amazon S3 bucket after a few objects were accidentally deleted by the development team.

Which of the following represent valid scenarios when a developer deletes an object in the versioning-enabled bucket? (Select two)
questions.simplifiedQuestion: What happens when you delete an object in a versioning-enabled S3 bucket? (Select two)
choices: A delete marker is set on the deleted object, but the actual object is not deleted
The delete marker has the same data associated with it, as the actual object
A delete marker has a key, version ID and Access Control List (ACL) associated with it
GET requests do not retrieve delete marker objects
GET requests can retrieve delete marker objects
correctAnswers: A delete marker is set on the deleted object, but the actual object is not deleted
GET requests do not retrieve delete marker objects
questions.explanation:
Delete Marker: In a versioning-enabled bucket, a DELETE request does not delete the object. Instead, Amazon S3 inserts a delete marker, which becomes the current object version. The actual object remains.

GET Requests: A simple GET request will not retrieve the delete marker object. The only way to list delete markers is by using the versions subresource in a GET Bucket versions request.

questions.question: After enabling S3 MFA-Delete, for which actions do you need MFA? (Select two)
questions.simplifiedQuestion: Which S3 actions require MFA when MFA-Delete is enabled? (Select two)
choices: Suspending versioning
Permanently delete an object version
Enabling Versioning
Uploading a new object version
Listing deleted versions
correctAnswers: Suspending versioning
Permanently delete an object version
questions.explanation:
MFA Delete requires additional authentication for either of the following operations:

- Change the versioning state of your bucket (e.g., suspending versioning)
- Permanently delete an object version

questions.question:
An automobile company manages its AWS resource creation and maintenance process through AWS CloudFormation. The company has successfully used CloudFormation so far, and wishes to continue using the service. However, while moving to CloudFormation, the company only moved critical resources and left out the other resources to be managed manually. To leverage the ease of creation and maintenance that CloudFormation offers, the company wants to move rest of the resources to CloudFormation.

Which of the following options is the recommended way to configure this requirement?
questions.simplifiedQuestion: How do you bring existing manually-created AWS resources under CloudFormation management?
choices: You can bring an existing resource into AWS CloudFormation management using resource import
You can use Mappings part of CloudFormation template to input the needed resources
Use Parameters section of CloudFormation template to input the required resources
Drift detection is the mechanism by which you add resources to the stack of Cloudformation resources already created
correctAnswers: You can bring an existing resource into AWS CloudFormation management using resource import
questions.explanation: If you created an AWS resource outside of AWS CloudFormation management, you can bring this existing resource into AWS CloudFormation management using resource import. You can manage your resources using AWS CloudFormation regardless of where they were created without having to delete and re-create them.

questions.question:
The Big Data team at an insurance company is performing a nightly ETL on top of your production RDS database to compute a view and then extract it into their data lake in Amazon S3. This query has been performing reasonably well in your website's infancy but now that it has grown in popularity, the query is running for a much longer period and affects the user experience while they browse your website.

How can you improve the situation in the short and long term?
questions.simplifiedQuestion: How do you offload a heavy ETL read query from a production RDS database?
choices: Use Athena to query RDS
Enable RDS Multi-AZ
Create an RDS Read Replica for the ETL team
Upgrade the RDS instance type
correctAnswers: Create an RDS Read Replica for the ETL team
questions.explanation: RDS Read Replicas make it easy to elastically scale out beyond the capacity constraints of a single DB instance for read-heavy workloads. For this use-case, you can use a read replica as the source for the ETL process, offloading the read traffic from the primary production database.

questions.question:
The Chief Technology Officer (CTO) of a healthcare company realized that he does not have access to an Amazon S3 bucket present in the company's own AWS account. The CTO is the root user for the AWS account and has created other AWS users using the root user account.

What is the reason for this behavior and how can you fix this?
questions.simplifiedQuestion: Why might the AWS root user be denied access to an S3 bucket in their own account?
choices: Root user has access to all the resources in his AWS account. Contact AWS support to resolve the access issue

Root user always has access to all the resources of the account. The Amazon S3 bucket could be from another AWS account and the S3 bucket has been shared with the root user and hence appears in his list of S3 buckets

An Amazon S3 bucket policy that specifies a wildcard (*) in the principal element, sometimes is declared void by AWS to avoid the risk of complete public exposure. Such S3 buckets policies are in invalid status and have random behavior

If an IAM user, with full access to IAM and Amazon S3, assigns a bucket policy to an Amazon S3 bucket and doesn't specify the AWS account root user as a principal, the root user is denied access to that bucket
correctAnswers: If an IAM user, with full access to IAM and Amazon S3, assigns a bucket policy to an Amazon S3 bucket and doesn't specify the AWS account root user as a principal, the root user is denied access to that bucket
questions.explanation: If an IAM user assigns a bucket policy that doesn't specify the root user as a principal, the root user can be denied access. The root user can still regain access by modifying the bucket policy to allow root user access, using the principal "AWS": "arn:aws:iam::123456789012:root".

questions.simplifiedQuestion: Why doesn't a CloudFormation stack policy grant users access to AWS resources?
choices: A stack policy applies only during stack updates, it doesn't provide access controls. The developer needs to provide access through IAM policies
Stack policies are associated with a particular IAM role or an IAM user. Hence, they only work for the users you have explicitly attached the policy to
The stack policy is invalid and hence the users are not granted any permissions. The developer needs to fix the syntactical errors in the policy
Stack policies do not allow wildcard character value (*) for the Principal element of the policy
correctAnswers: A stack policy applies only during stack updates, it doesn't provide access controls. The developer needs to provide access through IAM policies
questions.explanation: A stack policy is a JSON document that defines the update actions that can be performed on designated resources during a stack update. It does not provide access controls like an IAM policy. To control access to AWS resources or actions, you must use IAM.

questions.question:
A large IT company uses several AWS accounts for the different lines of business. Quite often, the CloudOps Engineer is faced with the problem of sharing AWS KMS keys across multiple AWS accounts for accessing AWS resources spread across these accounts.

How will you implement a solution to address this issue?
questions.simplifiedQuestion: How do you share a KMS key across multiple AWS accounts?
choices: The key policy for the KMS key must give the external account (or users and roles in the external account) permission to use the key. IAM policies in the external account must delegate the key policy permissions to its users and roles
AWS Owned key can be used across AWS accounts. Configure an AWS Owned key and use it across accounts that need to share the key material
Use AWS KMS service-linked roles to share access across AWS accounts
Declare a key policy for the KMS Key to give the external account permission to use the KMS key. This key policy should be embedded with the first request of every transaction
correctAnswers: The key policy for the KMS key must give the external account (or users and roles in the external account) permission to use the key. IAM policies in the external account must delegate the key policy permissions to its users and roles
questions.explanation:
To permit the usage of a KMS key to users and roles in another account, you must use two types of policies:

- The key policy in the owning account must give the external account permission to use the key.
- IAM policies in the external account must delegate those permissions to its users and roles.

questions.question:
A company wants to migrate a part of its on-premises infrastructure to AWS Cloud. As a starting point, the company is looking at moving their daily workflow files to AWS Cloud, such that the files are accessible from the on-premises systems as well as AWS Cloud. To reduce the management overhead, the company wants a fully managed service.

Which service/tool is the right choice for this requirement?
questions.simplifiedQuestion: Which AWS service provides NFS/SMB access to S3 with on-premises local caching?
choices: Amazon Elastic Block Store (Amazon EBS)
Amazon Simple Storage Service (Amazon S3)
Volume Gateway of AWS Storage Gateway
File Gateway of AWS Storage Gateway
correctAnswers: File Gateway of AWS Storage Gateway
questions.explanation: File Gateway presents a file-based interface to Amazon S3, appearing as a network file share. It enables you to store and retrieve Amazon S3 objects through standard file storage protocols (NFS or SMB). This provides low-latency performance by caching frequently accessed data on-premises, while storing data securely and durably in the cloud.

questions.question:
A CloudOps Engineer has configured Amazon EC2 instances in an Auto Scaling Group (ASG) for two separate development teams. However, only one configuration has CloudWatch agent installed on the instances, whereas the other one does not have it. The Engineer has not manually installed the agents on either group of instances.

Which of the following would you identify as a root-cause behind this issue?
questions.simplifiedQuestion: Why does one ASG have CloudWatch agent installed but another doesn't, without manual installation?
choices: The architecture of the InstanceType mentioned in your launch configuration does not match the image architecture. So, the ASG was created with errors, resulting in skipping CloudWatch agent. A thorough check is needed for such ASGs, more services could have been skipped
CloudWatch agent can be configured to be loaded on the EC2 instances while configuring the ASG. The developer could have unintentionally checked this flag on one of the ASGs he created

The instance architecture might not have been compatible with the AMI chosen. The incompatibility results in various errors, one of which is, some of the AWS services will not be installed as expected

If your AMI contains a CloudWatch agent, it's automatically installed on EC2 instances when you create an EC2 Auto Scaling group. The developer needs to choose the AMI that has CloudWatch agent pre-configured on it
correctAnswers: If your AMI contains a CloudWatch agent, it's automatically installed on EC2 instances when you create an EC2 Auto Scaling group. The developer needs to choose the AMI that has CloudWatch agent pre-configured on it
questions.explanation: If your AMI contains the CloudWatch agent, it is automatically installed on EC2 instances when you create an ASG. The most likely root cause is that one configuration used an AMI with the agent pre-installed, while the other used a stock AMI (e.g., stock Amazon Linux) that does not include it.

questions.question:
An organization that started as a single AWS account, gradually moved to a multi-account setup. The organization also has multiple AWS environments in each account, that were being managed at the account level. Backups are a big part of this management task. The organization is looking at moving to a centralized backup management process that consolidates and automates Cross-Region backup tasks across AWS accounts.

Which of the solutions below is the right choice for this requirement?
questions.simplifiedQuestion: How do you centralize and automate cross-account, cross-Region backups in AWS?
choices: Configure AWS Systems Manager Maintenance Windows to schedule backup tasks as per company's policies. Tag the resources to help identify them by the AWS environment they run in. Amazon CloudWatch dashboards hosted by Systems Manager to get an overall view of the status of all resources under the AWS account

Use Amazon EventBridge to create a workflow for scheduled backup of all AWS resources under an account. Amazon S3 lifecycle policies, Amazon EC2 instance backups, and Amazon RDS backups can be used to create the events for the EventBridge. The same workflow can be scheduled to work on production and non-production environments, based on the tags created

Create a backup plan in AWS Backup. Assign tags to resources based on the environment ( Production, Development, Testing). Create one backup policy for production environments and one backup policy for non-production environments. Schedule the backup plan based on the organization's backup policies

Use Amazon Data Lifecycle Manager to manage creation, deletion, and managing of all the AWS resources under an account. Tag all the resources that need to be backed up and use lifecycle policies to customize the backup management to cater to the needs of the organization
correctAnswers: Create a backup plan in AWS Backup. Assign tags to resources based on the environment ( Production, Development, Testing). Create one backup policy for production environments and one backup policy for non-production environments. Schedule the backup plan based on the organization's backup policies
questions.explanation: AWS Backup is a fully managed service that simplifies and automates data backup across AWS services. It leverages AWS Organizations to implement and maintain a central view of backup policy across resources in a multi-account environment. You can create backup plans and assign them to resources using tags.

questions.question:
A data analytics company runs its technology operations on AWS Cloud using different VPC configurations for each of its applications. A CloudOps Engineer wants to configure the Network Access Control List (ACL) and Security Group (SG) of VPC1 to allow access for AWS resources in VPC2.

Which is the best way of configuring this requirement?
questions.simplifiedQuestion: How do you configure Security Groups and NACLs to allow cross-VPC traffic?
choices: The Security Groups of instances on VPC1 should be configured to allow inbound traffic from resources in VPC2. By default, Network ACLs allow all inbound and outbound traffic. So, a default Network ACLs on VPC1 will not need any configuration changes

By default, Security Groups allow outbound traffic. Hence, only the inbound traffic configuration of the security groups have to be changed to allow requests from resources in VPC2 to access instances in VPC1. If the subnet is not associated with any Network ACL, you will not need any configuration changes

Network ACLs and Security Groups share a parent-child relationship. If resources in VPC2 are given inbound and outbound permissions on Network ACLs of VPC1, the resources will get necessary permissions on the associated security groups too

Based on the inbound and outbound traffic configurations on Network ACL of VPC1, you can create a similar deny rules on Security Groups of the instances in VPC1 to deny all traffic, other than the one originating from resources in VPC2
correctAnswers: The Security Groups of instances on VPC1 should be configured to allow inbound traffic from resources in VPC2. By default, Network ACLs allow all inbound and outbound traffic. So, a default Network ACLs on VPC1 will not need any configuration changes
questions.explanation: Security groups act at the instance level. You must update the security group on VPC1's instances to allow inbound traffic from VPC2's resources. Network ACLs are stateless and act at the subnet level. If you are using the default network ACL, it already allows all inbound and outbound traffic, so no changes are needed.

questions.question:
As a CloudOps Engineer, you have been asked to calculate the total network usage for all the EC2 instances of a company and determine which instance used the most bandwidth within a date range.

Which Amazon CloudWatch metric(s) will help you get the needed data?
questions.simplifiedQuestion: Which CloudWatch metrics measure total EC2 network bandwidth usage?
choices: NetworkTotalBytes
DataTransfer-Out-Bytes
DiskReadBytes and DiskWriteBytes
NetworkIn and NetworkOut
correctAnswers: NetworkIn and NetworkOut
questions.explanation: NetworkIn is the number of bytes received by the instance on all network interfaces. NetworkOut is the number of bytes sent out by the instance on all network interfaces. By aggregating these metrics over a date range, you can calculate total network usage and identify the instance with the highest bandwidth consumption.

questions.question:
A company is moving their on-premises technology infrastructure to AWS Cloud. Compliance rules and regulatory guidelines mandate the company to use its own software that needs socket level configurations. As the company is new to AWS Cloud, they have reached out to you for guidance on this requirement.

As an AWS Certified CloudOps Engineer, which option will you suggest for the given requirement?
questions.simplifiedQuestion: Which EC2 option supports per-socket or per-core software licensing?
choices: Opt for Reserved Instances that allow you to plan and help install the necessary software
Opt for Amazon EC2 Dedicated Host
Opt for Amazon EC2 Dedicated Instance
Opt for On-Demand instances that are highly available and require no prior planning
correctAnswers: Opt for Amazon EC2 Dedicated Host
questions.explanation: An Amazon EC2 Dedicated Host is a physical server fully dedicated to your use. Dedicated Hosts allow you to use your existing per-socket, per-core, or per-VM software licenses, which is necessary for software that requires socket-level configurations.

questions.question:
A CloudOps Engineer is configuring an Application Load Balancer (ALB) that fronts Amazon EC2 instances.

Which of the following options would you identify as correct for configuring the ALB? (Select two)
questions.simplifiedQuestion: Which statements about configuring an Application Load Balancer are correct? (Select two)
choices: You configure target groups of an ALB by attaching them to the listeners
When you create a listener, you define actions and conditions for the default rule
The targets of a target group in an ALB should all belong to the same Availability Zone
Before you start using your Application Load Balancer, you must add one or more listeners
A target can be registered with only one target group at any given time
correctAnswers: You configure target groups of an ALB by attaching them to the listeners
Before you start using your Application Load Balancer, you must add one or more listeners
questions.explanation:
Listeners: A listener checks for connection requests. You must add one or more listeners before using the ALB.

Target Groups: When you create a listener rule, you specify a target group and conditions. When a condition is met, traffic is forwarded to the corresponding target group.

questions.question:
A hospitality company runs their applications on its on-premises infrastructure but stores the critical customer data on AWS Cloud using AWS Storage Gateway. At a recent audit, the company has been asked if the customer data is secure while in-transit and at rest in the Cloud.

What is the correct answer to the auditor's question? And what should the company change to meet the security requirements?
questions.simplifiedQuestion: How does AWS Storage Gateway encrypt data in transit and at rest?
choices: AWS Storage Gateway uses IPsec to encrypt data that is transferred between your gateway appliance and AWS storage. All three Gateway types store data in encrypted form at-rest

AWS Storage Gateway uses SSL/TLS (Secure Socket Layers/Transport Layer Security) to encrypt data that is transferred between your gateway appliance and AWS storage. File and Volume Gateway data stored on Amazon S3 is encrypted. Tape Gateway data cannot be encrypted at-rest

AWS Storage Gateway uses SSL/TLS (Secure Socket Layers/Transport Layer Security) to encrypt data that is transferred between your gateway appliance and AWS storage. By default, Storage Gateway uses Amazon S3-Managed Encryption Keys to server-side encrypt all data it stores in Amazon S3

AWS Storage Gateway uses IPsec to encrypt data that is transferred between your gateway appliance and AWS storage. File and Volume Gateway data stored on Amazon S3 is encrypted. Tape Gateway data cannot be encrypted at-rest
correctAnswers: AWS Storage Gateway uses SSL/TLS (Secure Socket Layers/Transport Layer Security) to encrypt data that is transferred between your gateway appliance and AWS storage. By default, Storage Gateway uses Amazon S3-Managed Encryption Keys to server-side encrypt all data it stores in Amazon S3
questions.explanation: Storage Gateway uses SSL/TLS to encrypt data in transit. By default, it uses SSE-S3 to encrypt all data at rest stored in Amazon S3. Optionally, you can configure SSE-KMS for encryption.

questions.question:
A media company runs its business on Amazon EC2 instances backed by Amazon S3 storage. The company is apprehensive about the consistent increase in costs incurred from S3 buckets. The company wants to make some decisions regarding data retention, storage, and deletion based on S3 usage and cost reports. As a CloudOps Engineer, you have been hired to develop a solution to track the costs incurred by each S3 bucket in the AWS account.

How will you configure this requirement?
questions.simplifiedQuestion: How do you track and report S3 costs per bucket?
choices: Add a common tag to each bucket. Activate the tag as a cost allocation tag. Use the AWS Cost Explorer to create a cost report for the tag

Use AWS Trusted Advisor's rich set of best practice checks to configure cost utilization for individual S3 buckets. Trusted Advisor also provides recommendations based on the findings derived from analyzing your AWS cloud architecture
Use AWS Simple Monthly Calculator to check the cost against each S3 bucket in your AWS account
Configure AWS Budgets to see the cost against each S3 bucket in the AWS account
correctAnswers: Add a common tag to each bucket. Activate the tag as a cost allocation tag. Use the AWS Cost Explorer to create a cost report for the tag
questions.explanation: To track costs per S3 bucket, add a unique tag to each bucket (e.g., BucketName). Then, activate the tag as a cost allocation tag in the Billing and Cost Management console. After activation, you can use AWS Cost Explorer to filter and group costs by that tag, generating a report for each bucket.

questions.question:
A retail company has complex AWS VPC architecture that is getting difficult to maintain. The company has decided to configure VPC flow logs to track the network traffic to analyze various traffic flow scenarios. The systems administration team has configured VPC flow logs for one of the VPCs, but it's not able to see any logs. After initial analysis, the team has been able to track the error. It says Access error and the administrator of the team wants to change the IAM Role defined in the flow log definition.

What is the correct way of configuration a solution for this issue so that the VPC flow logs can be operational?
questions.simplifiedQuestion: How do you fix a VPC Flow Log that has an IAM role access error?
choices: The error indicates an internal error has occurred in the flow logs service. Raise a service request with AWS
The error indicates that the IAM role does not have a trust relationship with the flow logs service. Change the trust relationship from flow log configuration

The error indicates IAM role is not correctly configured. After you've created a flow log, you cannot change its configuration. Instead, you need to delete the flow log and create a new one with the required configuration
The flow log is still in the process of being created. It sometimes takes almost 10 minutes to start the logs
correctAnswers: The error indicates IAM role is not correctly configured. After you've created a flow log, you cannot change its configuration. Instead, you need to delete the flow log and create a new one with the required configuration
questions.explanation: An access error means the IAM role is not configured correctly (insufficient permissions or no trust policy). After a flow log is created, you cannot change its configuration (e.g., the IAM role). To fix this, you must delete the existing flow log and create a new one with the correct IAM role configuration.
