questions.question:
You are in S3 and have deleted all the files in it. As you can see, the bucket is empty. You have tried to delete the bucket afterward and it fails with an error saying the bucket is not empty.

What's the issue?
questions.simplifiedQuestion: Why does S3 bucket deletion fail saying "not empty" even though no objects are visible?
choices: An S3 bucket policy is set up and it prevents bucket deletion
S3 versioning is enabled and delete markers are still present in the bucket
S3 is eventually consistent. Wait two minutes and retry, it will work then
Some files are in Glacier
correctAnswers: S3 versioning is enabled and delete markers are still present in the bucket
questions.explanation: If versioning is enabled on a bucket, deleting an object does not remove it permanently. Instead, Amazon S3 inserts a delete marker, which becomes the current object version. The bucket is not considered empty until these delete markers are also deleted.

questions.question:
Your RDS database sometimes can become unresponsive, failing health checks and you need your application to fail-over automatically and safely without losing any committed transactions.

Which options would you choose?
questions.simplifiedQuestion: How do you enable automatic RDS failover without losing committed transactions?
choices: Create an RDS read replica in the same region and an AWS lambda function to promote that replica as the main database when the main RDS database is down
Create an RDS read replica in a different region and an AWS lambda function to promote that replica as the main database when the main RDS database is down
Enable RDS Multi-AZ
Setup a CloudWatch alarm for DB RAM going over 90% and reboot the database then
correctAnswers: Enable RDS Multi-AZ
questions.explanation: RDS Multi-AZ deployments provide high availability and automatic failover. In a Multi-AZ deployment, RDS automatically provisions and maintains a synchronous standby replica in a different Availability Zone. If the primary instance fails, RDS automatically fails over to the standby without losing committed transactions.

questions.question:
A startup uses Amazon S3 buckets for storing their customer data. The company has defined different retention periods for different objects present in their Amazon S3 buckets, based on the compliance requirements. But, the retention rules do not seem to work as expected.

Which of the following points are important to remember when configuring retention periods for objects in Amazon S3 buckets (Select two)?
questions.simplifiedQuestion: What should you know about S3 Object Lock retention periods? (Select two)
choices: The bucket default settings will override any explicit retention mode or period you request on an object version
When you apply a retention period to an object version explicitly, you specify a Retain Until Date for the object version
Different versions of a single object can have different retention modes and periods
You cannot place a retention period on an object version through a bucket default setting
When you use bucket default settings, you specify a Retain Until Date for the object version
correctAnswers: When you apply a retention period to an object version explicitly, you specify a Retain Until Date for the object version
Different versions of a single object can have different retention modes and periods
questions.explanation:
Explicit Retention: When applying a retention period explicitly, you specify a Retain Until Date.

Different Versions: Retention periods apply to individual object versions. Different versions of the same object can have different retention modes and periods.

questions.question:
An analytics company generates reports for various client applications, some of which have critical data. As per the company's compliance guidelines, data has to be encrypted during data exchange, for all channels of communication. An Amazon S3 bucket is configured as a website endpoint and this is now being added as a custom origin for CloudFront.

How will you secure this channel, as per the company's requirements?
questions.simplifiedQuestion: Can CloudFront use HTTPS to communicate with an S3 website endpoint origin?
choices: Configure CloudFront that mandates viewers to use HTTPS to request objects from S3. Configure S3 bucket to support HTTPS communication only. This will force CloudFront to use HTTPS for communication between CloudFront and S3
Configure CloudFront to mandate viewers to use HTTPS to request objects from S3. However, CloudFront and S3 will use HTTP to communicate with each other
CloudFront always forwards requests to S3 by using the protocol that viewers used to submit the requests. So, we only need to configure CloudFront to mandate the use of HTTPS for users
Communication between CloudFront and Amazon S3 is always on HTTP protocol since the network used for communication is internal to AWS and is inherently secure
correctAnswers: Configure CloudFront to mandate viewers to use HTTPS to request objects from S3. However, CloudFront and S3 will use HTTP to communicate with each other
questions.explanation: If your S3 bucket is configured as a website endpoint, you cannot configure CloudFront to use HTTPS to communicate with your origin because S3 does not support HTTPS connections in that configuration. HTTPS between CloudFront and S3 is not supported when the S3 bucket is configured as a website endpoint.

questions.question: How should MFA-Delete be enabled on an S3 bucket?
questions.simplifiedQuestion: What account and tool are required to enable S3 MFA-Delete?
choices: Using the root account and the AWS Console
Using an admin IAM user and the AWS Console
Using the root account and the AWS CLI
Using an admin IAM user and the AWS CLI
correctAnswers: Using the root account and the AWS CLI
questions.explanation: Only the bucket owner (root account) can enable MFA Delete, and it can only be enabled via the AWS CLI (or API). It cannot be enabled through the AWS Management Console.

questions.question:
A large IT company manages several projects on AWS Cloud and has decided to use AWS X-Ray to trace application workflows. The company uses a plethora of AWS services like API Gateway, Amazon EC2 instances, Amazon S3 storage service, Elastic Load Balancers and AWS Lambda functions.

Which of the following should the company keep in mind while using AWS X-Ray for the AWS services they use?
questions.simplifiedQuestion: Which AWS service does NOT send trace data directly to X-Ray?
choices: AWS X-Ray cannot be used to trace your AWS Lambda functions since they are not integrated
You cannot use X-Ray to trace or analyze user requests to your Amazon API Gateway APIs
AWS X-Ray does not integrate with Amazon S3 and you need to use CloudTrail for tracking requests on S3
Application Load balancers do not send data to X-Ray
correctAnswers: Application Load balancers do not send data to X-Ray
questions.explanation: Elastic Load Balancing application load balancers add a trace ID to incoming HTTP requests in a header named X-Amzn-Trace-Id. However, load balancers themselves do not send data to X-Ray and do not appear as a node on your service map.

questions.question:
You have a production Postgres RDS database and a custom rule in AWS Config has been set up and shows that some connections established to your database are not encrypted.

How can you ensure all connections to RDS are encrypted?
questions.simplifiedQuestion: How do you force all connections to an RDS PostgreSQL database to use SSL?
choices: Review the DB parameter groups
Patch the database with the SSL/TLS Postgres Addon
Enable SSL connections from the RDS Console
Edit the security group rules
correctAnswers: Review the DB parameter groups
questions.explanation: You can enforce all connections to your RDS for PostgreSQL database instance to use SSL by enabling the rds.force_ssl parameter in the DB parameter group. This parameter is set to "0" (off) by default and must be changed to "1".

questions.question:
A team needs to create an AMI from their Amazon EC2 instances for use in another environment.

What is the right way to create an application-consistent AMI from existing EC2 instances?
questions.simplifiedQuestion: How do you create an application-consistent AMI that flushes all data to disk?
choices: Create the AMI by disabling the No reboot option
Create an EBS-backed AMI for application consistency
Create the AMI with No reboot option enabled
Create the AMI with Delete on termination enabled
correctAnswers: Create the AMI by disabling the No reboot option
questions.explanation: By default (with "No reboot" option disabled), Amazon EC2 shuts down the instance, takes snapshots of any attached volumes, creates the AMI, and then reboots the instance. This ensures all operating system buffers are flushed to disk, resulting in an application-consistent AMI.

questions.question:
You would like to replace your on-premise NFS v3 drive with something that will leverage the huge capacity of Amazon S3. You would like to ensure files that are commonly used are locally cached on-premises.

What should you use?
questions.simplifiedQuestion: Which AWS service replaces an on-premises NFS drive with S3 storage and local caching?
choices: EBS Drives
Volume Gateway
EFS
File Gateway
correctAnswers: File Gateway
questions.explanation: File Gateway provides a seamless way to connect to the cloud to store application data files as durable objects in Amazon S3. It offers SMB or NFS-based access to data in Amazon S3 with local caching. This makes it an ideal replacement for an on-premise NFS drive, leveraging S3's capacity while maintaining low-latency access to frequently used files via local cache.

questions.question:
You suspect some of your employees try to access files in S3 that they don't have access to.

How can you verify this is indeed the case without them noticing?
questions.simplifiedQuestion: How do you silently monitor unauthorized S3 access attempts?
choices: Restrict their IAM policies and look at CloudTrail logs
Use AWS Config to define compliance rules on these users
Enable S3 Access Logs and analyze them using Athena
Use a bucket policy
correctAnswers: Enable S3 Access Logs and analyze them using Athena
questions.explanation: Server access logging provides detailed records for the requests that are made to an S3 bucket. Each access log record provides details like the requester, request time, request action, and response status (e.g., 403 Access Denied). You can enable these logs and use Amazon Athena to query them and analyze access patterns for specific employees.

questions.question:
You are deploying an application and use the cfn-init and cfn-signal script to ensure the application is properly deployed before signaling to CloudFormation the success of your stack deployment. Right now, every time you deploy, CloudFormation completes successfully, even though the instance is still executing the cfn-init script.

As a CloudOps Engineer, which of the following would you identify as the root cause behind the issue?
questions.simplifiedQuestion: Why does CloudFormation mark the stack as complete before cfn-init finishes running?
choices: You forgot to include the cfn-signal command in your user data
You forgot the Wait Condition
You did not disable Rollbacks
You forgot to include a deletion policy
correctAnswers: You forgot the Wait Condition
questions.explanation: The cfn-signal script sends a signal to CloudFormation. To make CloudFormation wait for this signal before completing the stack creation, you must create a Wait Condition resource in your template. The Wait Condition pauses the stack creation until it receives the required number of success signals or the timeout period expires.

questions.question:
An organization has multiple AWS accounts to manage different lines of business. A user from the Finance account has to access reports stored in Amazon S3 buckets of two other AWS accounts (belonging to the HR and Audit departments) and copy these reports back to the S3 bucket in the Finance account. The user has requested the necessary permissions from the CloudOps Engineer to perform this task.

As a CloudOps Engineer, how will you configure a solution for this requirement?
questions.simplifiedQuestion: How do you set up cross-account S3 access between multiple AWS accounts?
choices: Create resource-based policies in the HR, Audit accounts that will allow the requester from the Finance account to access the respective S3 buckets
Create IAM roles in the HR, Audit accounts, which can be assumed by the user from the Finance account when the user needs to access the S3 buckets of the accounts
Create resource-level permissions in the HR, Audit accounts to allow access to respective S3 buckets for the user in the Finance account

Create identity-based IAM policy in the Finance account that allows the user to make a request to the S3 buckets in the HR and Audit accounts. Also, create resource-based IAM policies in the HR, Audit accounts that will allow the requester from the Finance account to access the respective S3 buckets
correctAnswers: Create identity-based IAM policy in the Finance account that allows the user to make a request to the S3 buckets in the HR and Audit accounts. Also, create resource-based IAM policies in the HR, Audit accounts that will allow the requester from the Finance account to access the respective S3 buckets
questions.explanation: For cross-account access, permissions are required in both accounts. An identity-based policy in the Finance account allows the user to make the request. A resource-based policy (S3 bucket policy) in the HR and Audit accounts must allow the Finance user to access the specific buckets. Both conditions must be met for the request to succeed.
